---
title: "Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence"
chapter_id: "benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "rmi"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "uat"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "tokenmana"
  - "moecot"
  - "road_to_agi"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `benchmaxxing`, `rmi`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `tokenmana`, `moecot`, `road_to_agi` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs a way to move capability claims through evidence without overfitting to fixed benchmarks.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Benchmarks can saturate, be gamed, erase regressions, or fail to represent real requirements.

## Core claim

[benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence.core, support: argument] Benchmark ratchets should preserve regressions, create harder frontiers, record residuals, and resist Goodhart pressure.

## Mechanism

- Track benchmark mastery thresholds and saturation.
- Generate regressions and hidden checks.
- Move residual failures into escrow and new frontier tasks.

## Interfaces

- Routing and SCFs use readiness gates.
- Evidence matrix records support movement.
- Changelog records evidence changes.

## Invariants

- No test success is claimed without execution.
- Synthetic and empirical evidence remain distinct.
- Regressions are preserved.

## Failure modes

- Benchmark overfitting.
- Hidden regression deletion.
- Claim support inflation.

## Minimal implementation

A benchmark ratchet record with frontier, mastery, residuals, regressions, and anti-Goodhart checks.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Saturation detection test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Hidden benchmark transfer test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Regression preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `uat` | Unified Adaptive Tribunal | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Multi-AI collaborative refinement/evaluation protocol. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `tokenmana` | TokenMana | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Regenerative capacity mechanisms for load-stable token pricing. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `road_to_agi` | Road To AGI | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Remaining work / roadmap context. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
